inclusion_criteria: Randomized controlled trials (individual or cluster) with a comparator of standard/usual care or no lifestyle intervention.

Population: Pregnant women with obesity (pre-pregnancy or early-pregnancy BMI ≥30 kg/m² or as defined by study) and their offspring assessed either in infancy (<2 months) or in early childhood (3–7 years).
Intervention: Preconception or antenatal lifestyle programs targeting diet, exercise/physical activity, or combined behavioral lifestyle components.

Outcomes: Offspring cardiac remodeling (structure such as interventricular septal thickness; function including systolic/diastolic) and/or cardiovascular parameters (blood pressure, resting heart rate, arterial stiffness/thickness) within the specified age windows.
exclusion_criteria: Non-randomized or non-original study designs (e.g., observational, quasi-experimental, single-arm studies, reviews, protocols, conference abstracts without sufficient data) or lacking a usual-care/no-intervention control.

Interventions initiated postpartum or involving non-lifestyle components only (e.g., pharmacologic agents such as metformin, supplements, bariatric surgery), or multi-component programs where the lifestyle effect cannot be isolated.

Wrong population: maternal obesity not present or not reported; mixed BMI samples without an analyzable obese subgroup; offspring outcomes measured only outside the specified windows (<2 months or 3–7 years).
Irrelevant outcomes: studies not reporting offspring cardiac structure/function or cardiovascular parameters (e.g., maternal-only outcomes, anthropometry-only without cardiovascular measures).
screening_criteria.description: Screening to identify RCTs testing preconception or antenatal lifestyle interventions in obese pregnancy with offspring cardiovascular outcomes measured in infancy (<2 months) or early childhood (3–7 years), compared with standard care/no intervention.

included_articles.title: Lifestyle intervention in obese pregnancy and cardiac remodelling in 3-year olds: children of the UPBEAT RCT.
The Effects of a Preconception Lifestyle Intervention on Childhood Cardiometabolic Health-Follow-Up of a Randomized Controlled Trial.
Adiposity and cardiovascular outcomes in three-year-old children of participants in UPBEAT, an RCT of a complex intervention in pregnant women with obesity.
A preconception lifestyle intervention in women with obesity and cardiovascular health in their children.
Preconception lifestyle intervention in women with obesity and echocardiographic indices of cardiovascular health in their children.
